content.problemStatement:
Given nnn (nnn is **even**), determine the number of **black cells** in an n×nn \times nn×n chessboard. 

Update the function given in the IDE to solve the problem.



Input Format
The only line of the input contains a single integer nnn.



Output Format
Output the number of black cells in an n×nn \times nn×n chessboard.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
There are 323232 black cells and 323232 white cells in an 8×88 \times 88×8 chessboard. So the answer is 323232.

code.C++:
# Solution as follows

def square(t):
    value1 = t * t
    return value1
    
def half(i):
    value2 = i // 2
    return(value2)

n = int(input())
result1 = square(n)
result2 = half(result1)
print(result2)
